free_text: The B-11(3He,d)12C reaction mechanism at 18 and 22 MeV
English translation
Target was prepared by spattering of alcohol suspension
of boron powder containing 99% of B-11 isotope onto a
gold backing with the thickness of 0.25 mg/cm2.
Deuterons were detected by dE-E telescope on
the basis of silicon counters with an angle resolution
of 2 degrees.
Absolute cross section errors were defined on
a known solid angle of the detector, target thickness
and a charge collected in the Faraday cap.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Subents
004, 005 were re-organized. Subent 006 added.
Target thickness  was 1 mg/cm2.
Statistical error
Fig.1,2 from Izv.Akad.Nauk,52(1988)996
SD: STATUS updated. Accuracy of digitized
data presentation revised.
Target thickness  was 1 mg/cm2.
Fig.3 from Izv.Akad.Nauk,52(1988)996
SD: STATUS updated. Accuracy of digitized
data presentation revised.
No explanation of relative units is given.
Two dE-E telescopes on the basis of silicon counters
with an angle resolution of 5 degrees were used.
Gamma rays were detected by scintillation
detector with NaI(Tl)crystal (63 mm diameter and
63 mm height). Angle resolution was + - 13 degrees.
ANG-AZ-RL is an azimuthal angle between reaction
planes = 180.degr.
A polar angle between He-3 beam and deuterons.
A polar angle between He-3 beam and
gamma-quantum.
Target thickness  was 2.2 mg/cm2.
Digitizing error for ANG2
Error from the graphic bars. No information
about source of uncertainty.
Fig.4 from Izv.Akad.Nauk,52(1988)996

Subent 006.  ANG-AZ-RL=180.degr. excluded from the
COMMON section and values at ANG1 multiplied by -1.
